mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes signal expanding consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when RSAFS exhibits positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean return in those months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and a federal funds rate above 4% generate a stagflationary drag that forces rapid demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months while FEDFUNDS > 4
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or a regression of returns on the interaction term yields a p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of accommodative monetary policy signals real output growth without offsetting rate-hike risk, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 1%, positive momentum in INDPRO coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in payroll employment exceeding 200 thousand signals an overheating labor market that raises expectations of imminent monetary tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with PAYEMS increases above 200 thousand show negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 1400 thousand units reflect strong construction demand and positive spillovers to durable goods and employment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1400, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return mean is not significantly positive versus the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with subdued consumer price inflation supports real consumption and profit margin stability, and their interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < 3
failure_condition: The joint signal generates positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or a model including the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction does not improve directional accuracy over a main-effects-only model (chi-squared p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP leads analysts to revise corporate earnings expectations upward, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 60% of observations.
